channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: The man who invented the word 'Brexit' on his bittersweet legacy
item.description: "I'm a quiz question these days. Who invented the word 'Brexit'? Is it Boris Johnson, Nigel Farage, or an obscure failed politician-solicitor called Peter Wilding?"

item.title: Brussels Fire Brigade issues tips on how to cope with the city's heatwave
item.description: As temperatures in Brussels soar this week, the city's firefighters have reminded citizens that swimming in the canal is strictly prohibited.

item.title: 'Open wound': The lasting impact of Brexit on business – and on Brussels
item.description: "There's still a scar on the institutional side when you speak to people. There's still an open wound that Britain left."

item.title: 'I wanted to quit after every single attempt – but the desire to have a child was stronger'
item.description: In an interview with The Brussels Times, Alexia Desmet told us about the ups and downs of going through fertility treatment in Belgium.

item.title: Brussels wants to make city centre 'more accessible, greener and more pleasant'
item.description: The area around the Brucity administrative centre is being revamped into a "high-quality" public space that is accessible to everyone.

item.title: Two shootings in one night in Brussels
item.description: Gunfire was heard in both Molenbeek-Saint-Jean and Jette on Saturday night - but the police have not found any suspects yet.

item.title: Kiosk Radio's epic 16-hour rave returns to Brussels this summer
item.description: For many fans of Brussels’ electronic music scene, summer would not quite feel complete without the annual collaboration between Kiosk Radio and Crevette Records.

item.title: 300 participants form human chain on World Refugee Day in Brussels
item.description: Refugees share harrowing stories as activists mark World Refugee Day, spotlighting systemic gaps in asylum protections.

item.title: Brussels' graffiti battle: Who pays to clean up the capital's walls?
item.description: While Brussels supports street art and commissions artists to create murals in controlled environments, the city fights against unwanted tagging and graffiti.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 19 - 21 June
item.description: With the summer solstice arriving this weekend, Brussels is making the most of the season's longest days. Here are our favourite picks!

item.title: Everything you need to know about Fête de la Musique in Brussels
item.description: On any given weekend in Brussels, live music isn't hard to find. But this weekend, it's everywhere.

item.title: An unusual walking tour through Brussels – Europe’s capital of exiles
item.description: Asylum has always been central to the identity of Belgium as an independent nation and to Brussels as the national and European capital.

item.title: Brussels to close all parks on Friday evening due to stormy weather
item.description: Residents are being warned to avoid areas with trees across the entire region, as gusts of wind are forecasted to reach speeds of 80 to 90 kilometres per hour.

item.title: One person injured in fire on Rue due Trône in Brussels
item.description: Emergency responders arrived on the scene in Ixelles at around 4:40 a.m, with the entire building declared uninhabitable from the fire.

item.title: Teenagers in Belgium most likely to grow up in workless households in EU
item.description: Belgium's teenagers are the most likely in the EU to grow up in households where adults barely work, according to figures published by Eurostat.

item.title: Partial dismantling of Brussels' historic Palais du Midi to officially go ahead
item.description: The Brussels Region has authorised the controversial dismantling of the heritage building to continue work on the stalled Metro 3 project.

item.title: Brussels wants new drug czar to tackle 'hardest problem city has faced since terror attacks'
item.description: The Brussels Minister-President confirmed there will be a drug commissioner after all amid a spate of drug-related explosions in Brussels.

item.title: European Citizen Action Service Day takes place in Brussels next week
item.description: On the agenda are a keynote speech by European Commissioner for Democracy Michael McGrath, several panel discussions, thematic sessions and workshops.

item.title: Threats against Securail staff, Brussels opens investigation
item.description: "The safety of our staff is our top priority."

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 19 to 21 June
item.description: From concerts in the park to puppet shows and midsummer garland workshops, there are plenty of family-friendly activities taking place this weekend.
